rationale: The design brief is implementable within the current codebase. All 5 work streams map cleanly to existing code structures. Work stream 1 (schema command group) follows the identical pattern used by the 15 existing command groups in main.ts — a new registerSchemaCommands() plus a new commands/schema.ts file. The SCHEMAS registry in schemas-embedded.ts already provides the complete schema map needed for both template and list subcommands. Work stream 2 (error hints) is a contained change to validationError() in output.ts — Ajv errors already include schemaPath and keyword, so extracting required/enum/properties from the SCHEMAS registry at error time is straightforward. Work stream 3 (envelope auto-injection) is the most nuanced stream but feasible: const fields are detectable from schema properties, and the evidence.ts command already demonstrates the pattern of force-overwriting metadata fields (lines 105-108). The enrichTimestamp() deduplication concern is real but bounded — enrichTimestamp mutates in-place and checks for existing values, so envelope injection running before validation (which runs before writeJsonFile) maintains correct ordering. Work stream 4 (output unification) is a low-risk refactor of warn() in output.ts. Work stream 5 (skill cleanup) returned no matches in the grep, suggesting inline schemas may have already been removed or were never present — this needs verification but is not blocking.
concerns: The 48-failure-patterns acceptance criterion (spec acceptance_criteria[5]) has no documented test list. Without a concrete, reproducible set of failure cases, this criterion is unverifiable. The design brief flags this as an unresolved assumption but does not propose a resolution — the task compiler should either collect these patterns from session logs or replace this criterion with a measurable proxy (e.g., schema template covers all 15 schema types, error messages include required fields for all validation failure types).

Envelope auto-injection ordering vs enrichTimestamp needs explicit sequencing: envelope fields must be injected into the data object BEFORE passing to validate(), but enrichTimestamp runs inside writeJsonFile() AFTER validation. This means created_at will be injected twice — once by envelope logic (pre-validation) and once by enrichTimestamp (pre-write). The brief mentions deduplication but does not specify which layer wins. Recommendation: envelope injects a placeholder or skips created_at entirely, letting enrichTimestamp remain the single owner of timestamp injection.

The $ref resolution for template generation is bounded (2 external defs + 1 self-ref) but the implementation needs to handle the record.schema.json self-referential $defs/tierResult pattern. This is a local $ref (not cross-file), so Ajv does not help — template generation must walk $defs manually. Not complex, but the brief does not call out the local vs external $ref distinction.

Work stream 5 (skill inline schema removal) may be a no-op. Grep for schema/template patterns in plugin/skills/*.md returned zero matches. If skills already lack inline schemas, this work stream needs rescoping or explicit verification of what content to remove and what CLI template call instructions to add.
